notes: DISCRIMINATING ASSERTION: 'Uses exchange_calendars' is the only assertion where with_skill wins (1/1 pass vs 0/1 baseline). This is the skill's primary value — steering toward the production-grade library.

NON-DISCRIMINATING: Eval 1 (holiday detection) and Eval 2 (upgrade path) pass all assertions in BOTH configurations. The baseline agent independently found exchange_calendars for these tasks, likely because the prompts mention 'no manual list' and 'production-grade' which are strong enough cues.

QUALITATIVE DIFFERENCE (not captured by assertions): The with-skill outputs follow the exact SessionDetector + ExchangeConfig pattern from the validated exness-data-preprocess codebase. The without-skill outputs use varied approaches (bdate_range difference, SessionWindow dataclass, etc.). The skill produces more consistent, copy-pasteable code.
TOKEN COST: With-skill runs cost ~6% more tokens on average (56K vs 53K), and ~46% more time (354s vs 243s). The time delta is partly due to the skill-reading overhead.

EVAL 0 BASELINE: The baseline produced a FASTER but LESS CAPABLE solution — pure pandas vectorized math (no holidays, no early closes). Faster to write and execute, but misses the production accuracy the skill targets.
